Title: Successful user registration and navigation to homepage
Preconditions:
- User is on the registration page.
- System is accessible and not under maintenance.
Test Data:
- Valid username: `testuser123`
- Valid email: `testuser@example.com`
- Valid password: `SecurePass123!`
Test Steps:
1. User enters valid username, email, and password.
2. User submits the registration form.
3. System validates inputs and confirms registration success.
4. User is redirected to the homepage.
Expected Result:
- User sees "Utente registrato con successo" confirmation.
- User is navigated to the homepage without errors.

Title: User registration failure due to missing fields
Preconditions: - User is on the registration page.
Test Data:
- Empty username field.
- Valid email: `testuser@example.com`
- Valid password: `SecurePass123!`
Test Steps:
1. User leaves the username field empty.
2. User submits the registration form.
3. System detects missing field.
Expected Result:
- User sees an error message: "Username is required."
- User is not redirected to the homepage.

Title: User registration failure due to invalid email format
Preconditions: - User is on the registration page.
Test Data:
- Valid username: `testuser123`
- Invalid email: `invalid-email`
- Valid password: `SecurePass123!`
Test Steps:
1. User enters an invalid email format.
2. User submits the registration form.
3. System validates email format.
Expected Result:
- User sees an error message: "Invalid email format."
- User is not redirected to the homepage.

Title: Successful navigation from Home to Impostazioni via Le mie segnalazioni
Preconditions: - User is logged in and on the homepage.
Test Steps:
1. User clicks on the lateral menu.
2. User selects "Homepage."
3. User clicks on "Le mie segnalazioni."
4. User clicks on "Impostazioni."
5. System closes the menu and opens the schema.
Expected Result:
- User successfully navigates to the Impostazioni page.
- Menu closes, and the schema opens without errors.

Title: Navigation failure due to missing menu item
Preconditions: - User is logged in and on the homepage.
Test Steps:
1. User clicks on the lateral menu.
2. User attempts to click on a non-existent menu item (e.g., "FakeOption").
Expected Result:
- System displays an error: "Menu item not found."
- User remains on the homepage.

Title: Successful addition of an alert with category selection
Preconditions:
- User is on the "Add Alert" page.
- First 4 fields are mandatory (e.g., Title, Description, Location, Severity).
Test Data:
- Valid title: `Broken Streetlight`
- Valid description: `Streetlight not working on Maple Ave.`
- Valid location: `123 Maple Ave.`
- Valid severity: `High`
Test Steps:
1. User clicks "Add Alert" in the lateral menu.
2. User clicks "Add Category."
3. System opens the category list.
4. User selects a category (e.g., "Infrastructure").
5. User fills in the first 4 fields correctly.
6. User submits the alert.
Expected Result:
- Alert is created successfully.
- User sees confirmation: "Alert added successfully."

Title: Alert creation failure due to incomplete fields
Preconditions: - User is on the "Add Alert" page.
Test Data:
- Empty title field.
- Valid description: `Streetlight not working.`
- Valid location: `123 Maple Ave.`
- Valid severity: `High`
Test Steps:
1. User clicks "Add Alert."
2. User leaves the title field empty.
3. User attempts to submit the alert.
Expected Result:
- System displays an error: "Title is required."
- Alert is not created.

Title: Successful viewing of all alerts and selection of one alert
Preconditions:
- User is on the "View Alerts" page.
- System has at least 3 alerts in the database.
Test Steps:
1. User clicks "View Alerts."
2. System displays all alerts.
3. User selects one alert from the list.
4. System retrieves and displays the selected alert.
Expected Result:
- All alerts are displayed correctly.
- Selected alert details are shown without errors.

Title: Viewing alerts when no alerts exist
Preconditions:
- User is on the "View Alerts" page.
- System has no alerts in the database.
Test Steps:
1. User clicks "View Alerts."
2. System checks for alerts.
Expected Result:
- System displays: "No alerts found."
- User is not allowed to select an alert.

Title: Repeated viewing of all alerts
Preconditions:
- User is on the "View Alerts" page.
- System has 5 alerts.
Test Steps: 1. User clicks "View Alerts" 3 times in succession.
Expected Result:
- System refreshes the alert list each time without errors.
- No duplicate alerts or crashes occur.

Title: Successful profile editing and saving changes
Preconditions: - User is logged in and on the profile page.
Test Data:
- Valid name: `John Doe`
- Valid address: `456 Oak St.`
- Valid phone: `+1234567890`
Test Steps:
1. User clicks "Profile" in the lateral menu.
2. User clicks the blue house icon.
3. User navigates to the "abitazioni" (housing) screen.
4. User fills in all fields correctly.
5. User clicks "Save Changes."
Expected Result:
- System validates fields and saves changes.
- User sees confirmation: "Profile updated successfully."

Title: Profile editing failure due to invalid phone format
Preconditions: - User is logged in and on the profile page.
Test Data:
- Valid name: `John Doe`
- Valid address: `456 Oak St.`
- Invalid phone: `123`
Test Steps:
1. User enters an invalid phone number.
2. User clicks "Save Changes."
Expected Result:
- System displays an error: "Invalid phone format."
- Changes are not saved.

Title: Profile editing with partial corrections
Preconditions:
- User is logged in and on the profile page.
- User initially submits incomplete data.
Test Steps:
1. User leaves the address field empty.
2. User clicks "Save Changes."
3. System detects incomplete data.
4. User corrects the address field.
5. User clicks "Save Changes" again.
Expected Result:
- System prompts: "Please correct the following fields: Address."
- After correction, changes are saved successfully.

Title: Discarding profile changes
Preconditions:
- User is logged in and on the profile page.
- User has made changes to the profile.
Test Steps:
1. User edits the profile (e.g., changes name).
2. User clicks "Discard Changes."
Expected Result:
- All changes are reverted.
- User sees confirmation: "Changes discarded successfully."
- Original profile data is restored.
